text.title: Spec Quality Gates
text.summary: The four-dimension quality gate (Completeness, Clarity, Consistency, Context) that a spec must pass before moving to the design phase, with common failure modes and fixes.

rule.statement: All required sections must be present and non-empty. Problem Statement must have all five fields (Who, Problem, Impact, Solution, Success Metric). FR table must have ID, Requirement, Traces To, and Priority for every row. AC must have Given, When, Then for every scenario.
rule.rationale: Missing sections are not optional — each carries information the design phase requires. An empty "Open Questions" section is worse than no section: it signals the questions were asked but not answered.

antipattern.description: Open Questions section left populated (unresolved questions still present) when Status is set to Approved.
antipattern.reason: Unresolved questions are blocker items, not optional notes. They must be resolved before design can begin.

rule.statement: Reject any requirement containing these ambiguous words: good, fast, easy, appropriate, reasonable, support, handle, manage, modern, user-friendly. Replace with measurable criteria.
rule.rationale: Ambiguous requirements produce ambiguous ACs, which produce untestable code. Each ambiguous word is a decision deferred to the developer or agent, who may choose incorrectly.

rule.statement: Each requirement must have exactly one interpretation. If two team members would implement the same FR differently, the FR is too ambiguous and must be rewritten.
rule.rationale: Multiple interpretations mean the spec is not a contract. A spec that is not a contract provides no value over informal discussion.

rule.statement: Every FR-ID must be unique within the spec. Every AC must reference a real FR-ID. Every US must have at least one Must-priority FR. MoSCoW priorities must be assigned consistently — not all Must.
rule.rationale: Duplicate IDs and broken references cause executor agents to silently skip or mis-map requirements. All-Must priority is a sign of prioritization debt that will cause scope overrun.

rule.statement: The spec must reference constitution.md in the Reference Documents table. Related features in done/ must be identified and linked. Out of Scope must have at least 3 explicit exclusions with a timeline (Phase 2, Never, Future).
rule.rationale: Without constitution reference, agents may choose patterns that contradict project standards. Without related feature links, agents may duplicate existing implementations.

antipattern.description: NFRs stated as "The system should be fast and scalable."
antipattern.reason: No numeric target. Untestable. Cannot drive design decisions. Replace with "< 500ms p95" and "10k concurrent users".

antipattern.description: Recommended Skills section left stale from template without being updated after design choices are made.
antipattern.reason: Stale skill references route agents to wrong methodologies. Update after design.md is written.

antipattern.description: Related Features table left empty when features/done/ contains clearly related work.
antipattern.reason: Agents will duplicate existing implementations rather than reusing patterns from completed work.
